events.title: Kotor Green Market — Saturday Edition
events.description: The weekly green market outside the city walls expands on Saturdays with cheese and prosciutto producers from Njeguši, honey from Lovćen and seasonal fruit from the Crmnica valley.

events.title: Sunrise Hike: Ladder of Kotor
events.description: Guided sunrise hike up the historic Ladder of Kotor trail with views over the bay. Moderate fitness required; around 3 hours round trip with a coffee stop at the old fort.

events.title: Klapa Singing Evening
events.description: Traditional Dalmatian-Boka klapa a cappella groups perform in the acoustically stunning church of St Spirit. Part of the lead-up to the annual klapa festival in Perast.

events.title: Kotor Cats Day
events.description: A light-hearted celebration of Kotor's famous cats with a charity market, kids' drawing workshop and adoption stand run by the local animal welfare association.

events.title: Montenegrin Wine Tasting in the Old Town
events.description: Guided tasting of six Montenegrin wines — Vranac, Krstač and coastal varieties — with cheese pairings, hosted in a 16th-century palazzo cellar.

events.title: KotorArt Festival — Opening Concert
events.description: The international KotorArt festival opens with a symphonic concert in front of St Tryphon Cathedral. One of the most prestigious classical music events in the region, drawing performers from across Europe.

events.title: Boka Bay Sailing Regatta
events.description: Amateur and club sailing regatta across the inner bay between Kotor and Perast. Spectators can watch from the waterfront; awards ceremony at the marina in the evening.

events.title: Bokeljska Noć — Night of the Boka
events.description: Kotor's most beloved tradition: decorated boats parade through the bay at night, followed by music and celebration on the waterfront. The whole town turns out for this spectacular event.
